violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Milk and half and half were observed held at 42-43 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. At the time of inspection, food was determined safe based on time and temperature of the products. Foods moved to different refrigeration to rapidly cool. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The walk-in cooler was observed maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit. The ambient temperature of the refrigeration must be 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. A maintenance service was in the process at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
